title: antirhea affinis
parts: Antirhea affinis has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and bark, which are used in traditional medicine. The leaves of the Antirhea affinis plant contain medicinal constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant are rich in glycosides and terpenoids, which are believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the bark of Antirhea affinis contains tannins and alkaloids, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever and skin infections.
side_effects: Antirhea affinis can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe gastrointestinal issues, including diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal pain. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant may also lead to cardiovascular complications, including hypertension and cardiac arrhythmias. Additionally, some individuals may experience allergic reactions, such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing, after coming into contact with or ingesting the plant. In severe cases, improper use of Antirhea affinis may even cause kidney or liver damage.
